value.Title: Terrestrial Energy Appoints Tyler Gronbach as Vice President of Investor Relations and Public Relations Seasoned external affairs executive to lead investor and public communications as company advances IMSR commercialization

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-M2K3-SG5M-90VG-00000-00Terrestrial Energy Appoints Tyler Gronbach as Vice President of Investor Relations and Public Relations2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisTerrestrial Energy Appoints Tyler Gronbach as Vice President of Investor Relations and Public RelationsSeasoned external affairs executive to lead investor and public communications as company advances IMSR commercializationCHARLOTTE, N.C.

Terrestrial Energy Inc. (Nasdaq: IMSR) ("Terrestrial Energy" or the "Company"), a developer of proprietary Generation IV small modular nuclear power plants, today announced that Tyler Gronbach has joined the company as Vice President of Investor Relations and Public Relations.

The appointment comes as Terrestrial Energy accelerates commercialization of its Integral Molten Salt Reactor (IMSR) and expands engagement with institutional investors, sell-side analysts and the financial media following its Nasdaq listing last year. Gronbach will report to CEO Simon Irish and lead the company's IR and PR functions.

"A step-change in commerical performance of Nuclear energy supply will drive its rapid expansion to meet many clear market and policy needs. Our IMSR Plant delivers that, as fission technology choices are fundamental to performance," said Simon Irish, CEO of Terrestrial Energy. "Tyler brings decades of experience connecting technology and business solutions with investors and the media. He joins the company to ensure that our messaging captures the energy market value of IMSR technology at a time when capital markets are evolving rapidly to price technology fundamentals."

Gronbach joins Terrestrial Energy from Wolfspeed, Inc. (NYSE: WOLF), where he served as Vice President of External Affairs. At Wolfspeed, he designed and executed an integrated communications platform that supported the company's transformation from a legacy LED business into a silicon carbide leader serving electric vehicle, industrial and renewable energy markets. His work there helped attract national media attention for the company's strategic pivot, organized a U.S. Presidential visit and supported more than $6.5 billion in capital raises.

"Terrestrial Energy holds one of the most exciting innnovation stories in the tech sector today - transformative clean, firm energy supply at massive scale. The timing is extraordinary for the nuclear sector," said Gronbach. "The IMSR's fission technology and plant design advantages deliver the transformative nuclear energy supply that government and industry are demanding. It's a compelling story, and I'm eager to put my experience to work telling it."

Previously, Gronbach served as Vice President of Communications for Pharma Services at Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE: TMO), where he led global internal and external communications for a $3 billion business segment. His career spans decades of external affairs work across corporate communications, investor relations, government affairs and marketing for publicly traded companies in the technology, life sciences and energy sectors.

About Terrestrial Energy

Terrestrial Energy is a developer of Generation IV nuclear plants that use its proprietary Integral Molten Salt Reactor (IMSR). The IMSR captures the transformative operating benefits of molten salt reactor technology in a plant design that represents true innovation in capital efficiency, cost reduction, versatility and functionality of nuclear energy supply. IMSR plants are designed to be small and modular for distributed supply of low-cost, reliable, dispatchable, clean, high-temperature industrial heat and electricity and to be customized for a dual-use energy role relevant to many industrial applications, such as petrochemical and chemical synthesis and data center operation. In so doing, IMSR plants extend the application of nuclear energy far beyond electric power markets. Their deployment will support the rapid growth of clean firm heat and power, delivering energy self-reliance, grid reliability and economic growth. Terrestrial Energy uses an innovative plant design together with proven and demonstrated molten salt reactor technology and readily available and inexpensive standard-assay low-enriched uranium in its fuel for a nuclear plant with a unique set of operating characteristics and compelling transformative commercial potential. Terrestrial Energy is engaged with regulators, suppliers, industrial partners and energy end users to build, license and commission the first IMSR plants in the early 2030s.

Forward-Looking Statements

The statements contained in this press release that are not purely historical are forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements include, but are not limited to, statements regarding our expectations, milestones, hopes, beliefs, intentions or strategies regarding the future. In addition, any statements that refer to projections, forecasts or other characterizations of future events or circumstances, including any underlying assumptions, are forward-looking statements. The words "anticipate," "believe," "continue," "could," "estimate," "expect," "intends," "may," "might," "plan," "possible," "potential," "predict," "project," "should," "will," "would" and similar expressions may identify forward-looking statements, but the absence of these words does not mean that a statement is not forward-looking.

The forward-looking statements contained in this press release are based on our current expectations and beliefs concerning future developments and their potential effects on the Company. There can be no assurance that future developments affecting the Company will be those that we have anticipated. These forward-looking statements speak only as of the date of this press release and involve a number of risks, uncertainties (some of which are beyond our control) or other assumptions that may cause actual results or performance to differ materially from those expressed or implied by these forward-looking statements. Factors that may cause actual results to differ materially from current expectations include, but are not limited to: (1) risks related to the development, manufacturing and construction of IMSR Plants and key components, including potential delays, cost overruns and contractor performance issues; (2) the Company's ability to obtain applicable regulatory approvals and licenses on a timely basis or at all; (3) the ability of management to manage growth; (4) the possibility that the Company may be adversely affected by other economic, business, and/or competitive factors, including from alternative energy technologies, energy price volatility, and competition from other advanced reactor developers; (5) potential supply chain constraints and cost inflation for specialized nuclear-grade materials and components; (6) any failure to comply with the laws and regulations governing the use, transportation, and disposal of toxic, hazardous and/or radioactive materials; (7) changes in domestic and foreign business, market, financial and political conditions, and in applicable laws and regulations, including tariffs; (8) the ability to raise additional funding in the future; (9) the outcome of any legal proceedings that may be instituted against the Company; and (10) other risk factors described herein as well as the risk factors and uncertainties described in the documents filed by the Company from time to time with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC").

urn:contentItem:6JH0-1DY3-RV6X-J37J-00000-00American Electric Power's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisAmerican Electric Power's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

American Electric Power's trailing twelve months dividend is $3.76. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.74%. Its current price is at a 0.5% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 44.1% premium to its 52 week low. If American Electric Power was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.95%.

In comparison, Dominion Energy, Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $2.67. Its dividend yield at its current price is 4.14%. Its current price is at a 3.5% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 28.5% premium to its 52 week low. If Dominion Energy, Inc. was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 5.32%.

$1000 invested into American Electric Power stock 5 years ago would be worth $2,176 today. This total gain of $1,176 is generated by a capital gain of $849 and dividends of $326.

$1000 invested into Dominion Energy, Inc. stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,243 today. This total gain of $243 is generated by a capital gain of $18 and dividends of $224.

American Electric Power's dividend has grown 5.3% over the last 4 years.

Dominion Energy, Inc.'s dividend has grown 0% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) is a regulated electric utility serving approximately 5.6 million customers across 11 U.S. states. The company generates the largest portion of its revenue from retail electric sales within its Vertically Integrated Utilities and Transmission & Distribution Utilities segments. In 2025, these segments contributed significantly to the $21.88 billion in total revenues, with regulated rates on residential, commercial, and industrial loads forming the core. The fastest-growing area was commercial retail within Transmission & Distribution, which saw a 27.8% year-to-date increase, driven by demand from data centers and hyperscalers. Incremental transmission investments and wholesale activities further supported overall load expansion.

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

Company Profile

Exelon Corporation operates as a holding company for regulated electric and gas utilities, primarily generating revenue through electricity transmission and distribution, which accounts for approximately 85% of total revenue. The largest revenue contributors are its PHI subsidiary, focused on Mid-Atlantic operations, and ComEd, serving northern Illinois, which together represent over 58% of the company's $24.3 billion full-year revenue. The fastest-growing segments include BGE, driven by Maryland distribution rates, and PECO, supported by Pennsylvania electric and gas rates and favorable weather conditions. These segments have shown significant year-over-year growth, reflecting the company's investments in infrastructure and regulatory recoveries.

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

Constellation Energy Corporation's trailing twelve months dividend is $1.59. Its dividend yield at its current price is 0.51%. Its current price is at a 24% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 36.6% premium to its 52 week low. If Constellation Energy Corporation was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 0.69%.

In comparison, Dominion Energy, Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $2.67. Its dividend yield at its current price is 4.14%. Its current price is at a 3.5% discount to its 52 week high. Company Profile

Constellation Energy Corporation is the largest producer of clean, reliable electricity in the United States, operating a 55 GW fleet that includes nuclear, natural gas, geothermal, hydro, wind, and solar facilities. Approximately 80% of its output comes from wholesale electricity generation, primarily nuclear, which serves as the core revenue source. The company also provides competitive retail supply to 2.5 million customers, including three-fourths of the Fortune 100. Its fastest-growing segments include power agreements with data centers, driven by increasing demand from AI and electrification, and the recent Calpine acquisition, which expands its natural gas and geothermal capacity.

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

Its current price is at a 28.5% premium to its 52 week low. If Dominion Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 5.32%.

In comparison, Xcel Energy Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $2.30. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.78%. Its current price is at a 0.8% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 30.1% premium to its 52 week low. If Xcel Energy Inc. was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.61%.

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy operates as a regulated utility, providing electricity and natural gas services across eight U.S. states. Electric revenues represent the largest source of income, contributing $12.16 billion, or 83% of total operating revenues in 2025. These revenues are primarily driven by residential, commercial, and industrial sales. Natural gas revenues, accounting for $2.45 billion or 17% of total revenues, serve heating and industrial needs. The fastest-growing segments include electric sales, which increased by 2% on a weather-normalized basis, fueled by demand from commercial and industrial sectors, particularly in the energy sector and emerging data center loads. The company's infrastructure investments in renewables and transmission further support its long-term growth strategy.

value.Title: Xcel Energy 's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSND-R4W0-00000-00Xcel Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisXcel Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

Xcel Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $2.30. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.78%. Its current price is at a 0.8% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 30.1% premium to its 52 week low. If Xcel Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.61%.

In comparison, Dominion Energy, Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $2.67. Its dividend yield at its current price is 4.14%. Its current price is at a 3.5% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 28.5% premium to its 52 week low. If Dominion Energy, Inc. was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 5.32%.

$1000 invested into Xcel Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,596 today. This total gain of $596 is generated by a capital gain of $378 and dividends of $218.

$1000 invested into Dominion Energy, Inc. stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,243 today. This total gain of $243 is generated by a capital gain of $18 and dividends of $224.

Xcel Energy's dividend has grown 5.1% over the last 4 years.

Dominion Energy, Inc.'s dividend has grown 0% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

Xcel Energy operates as a regulated utility, providing electricity and natural gas services across eight U.S. states. Electric revenues represent the largest source of income, contributing $12.16 billion, or 83% of total operating revenues in 2025. These revenues are primarily driven by residential, commercial, and industrial sales. Natural gas revenues, accounting for $2.45 billion or 17% of total revenues, serve heating and industrial needs. The fastest-growing segments include electric sales, which increased by 2% on a weather-normalized basis, fueled by demand from commercial and industrial sectors, particularly in the energy sector and emerging data center loads. The company's infrastructure investments in renewables and transmission further support its long-term growth strategy.

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

value.Title: The Beat: Innovation awards, Niron's Sartell expansion, Largest software developers

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-8P73-RTPY-C0V5-00000-00The Beat: Innovation awards, Niron's Sartell expansion, Largest software developers2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:12ZLexisNexisThe Beat: Innovation awards, Niron's Sartell expansion, Largest software developersAlyxandra Sego

Welcome to Minne Inno's The Beat, a weekly look at the people, companies and ideas that are shaping the Twin Cities' innovation economy. Subscribe to get The Beat newsletter sent straight to your inbox every Thursday afternoon.

Alyxandra: Hello Beat readers,

Innovators win honors among 2026 Minnesota Manufacturing Awards

The Minneapolis/St. Paul Business Journal has named the innovation winners in its Minnesota Manufacturing Awards, recognizing a handful of trail-blazing leaders in the state.

Swinergy Inc. won Product Innovation of the Year in the small-company category, while Niron Magnetics took the top honor among midsize firms.

Anne Hed, CEO of Roseville-based Hed Cycling Products, is the 2026 Career Achievement honoree. A former professional triathlete, she co-founded the company in the 1980s and helped pioneer aerodynamic cycling wheels for competitive bikers. 

Swinergy, a Minneapolis startup founded in 2022 by CEO Eugene Alvey, developed an anaerobic digestion system that converts hog waste into renewable natural gas. The company is building its first commercial system in southern Minnesota and plans additional facilities. Its work has earned honors including the 2025 MN Cup renewable energy award and the Business Journal's 40 under 40 honorees this year. 

Niron Magnetics, a University of Minnesota spinout, is scaling production of rare-earth-free magnets using iron-nitride technology. The Minneapolis-based company has raised more than $300 million and is planning a 1.6 million-square-foot manufacturing plant. Niron is currently building its first plant in Sartell that would produce about 1,500 tons of magnets annually.

The manufacturing awards program honors eight companies and three leaders. Winners will be featured in a July 17 special section.

Niron Magnetics expands Sartell plant to 287,000 square feet in design overhaul

Niron Magnetics Inc. plans to increase the size of the manufacturing plant currently under construction in Sartell to about 287,000 square feet when fully built, according to an environmental assessment worksheet outlining the plans.

Niron's original plans for the Sartell plant outlined a 190,000-square-foot facility that would start production in mid-2027. 

Read more here. 

Largest software development firms in the Twin Cities, 2026

The Twin Cities' largest software development firms for 2026 have been ranked by the Minneapolis/St. Paul Business Journal.

Topping the list is Fortra, headquartered in Eden Prairie. The firm employs 2,383 software developers and specializes in cybersecurity. Matthew Reck leads the company.

Second on the list is Jamf Holding Corp., located in Minneapolis. With 2,322 software developers, the company focuses on IT services. Beth Tschida serves as the top executive.

Other notable firms include SPS Commerce Inc., which ranks third, Horizontal, which ranks fourth and Perforce Software, which rounds out the top five. 

See the full list here.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-MD93-S846-S3HK-00000-00Wolfspeed Stock Surges 23% on AI Infrastructure Hype and Short Squeeze Momentum2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisWolfspeed Stock Surges 23% on AI Infrastructure Hype and Short Squeeze Momentum

Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Mike Clair

May 13th, 2026 ( International Business Times Australia - Delivered by Newstex )

NEW YORK - Wolfspeed Inc. shares rocketed more than 23% in early trading Wednesday, climbing to $66.35 as a bullish research note spotlighting the silicon carbide specialist as a prime AI infrastructure play triggered heavy buying and accelerated a short squeeze in the heavily shorted name.

Wolfspeed Stock Surges 23% on AI Infrastructure Hype and Short Squeeze Momentum

The North Carolina-based company, a leader in silicon carbide semiconductors critical for electric vehicles, renewable energy and high-power AI data center applications, has been on a tear since emerging from Chapter 11 bankruptcy last fall with a dramatically cleaned-up balance sheet. Wednesday's surge extended a seven-session winning streak and pushed the stock to fresh post-restructuring highs.

Analysts at Citrini Research published a note late Tuesday positioning Wolfspeed as one of the clearest "laggard catch-up" opportunities in the semiconductor sector. The report highlighted the company's fully U.S.-based supply chain, restructured factories optimized for high-efficiency power semiconductors, and accelerating revenue from AI-related demand as key differentiators.

Silicon carbide chips excel at handling high voltages and temperatures with greater efficiency than traditional silicon, making them essential for power-hungry AI servers, data center infrastructure and next-generation EVs. Wolfspeed's technology addresses exactly the bottlenecks facing hyperscalers racing to deploy massive GPU clusters.

Short interest has remained elevated, with recent data showing more than 57% of the float sold short in some measurements, creating fertile ground for a squeeze. As the stock climbed on the Citrini catalyst, forced covering amplified the move, with volume spiking dramatically in pre-market and early sessions.

The rally builds on positive momentum from Wolfspeed's fiscal third-quarter results released May 5. Although the company reported a revenue miss and continued negative gross margins due to factory underutilization, investors focused on forward-looking signals: AI sales up 30%, debt reduction progress, cash reserves near $1.2 billion and strategic refinancing that slashed interest expenses.

Post-bankruptcy leadership changes have also boosted confidence. CEO Robert Feurle and a refreshed executive team have emphasized operational execution, capacity expansion and monetizing tax credits. The company recently received nearly $700 million in IRS refunds under advanced manufacturing investment credits, further strengthening liquidity.

Wolfspeed's long-term thesis centers on the structural shift toward wide-bandgap semiconductors. Global demand for silicon carbide is projected to grow rapidly as AI energy consumption soars and electrification accelerates. The company's Mohawk Valley and other U.S. fabs position it to capture domestic content advantages amid supply chain security concerns.

Challenges remain significant. Wolfspeed continues operating with negative margins amid ramp-up costs and underutilized capacity. Competition in the silicon carbide space is intensifying from larger players, and execution on production scaling will determine whether the current enthusiasm translates into sustainable profitability.

Analysts maintain a range of views. Some see substantial upside if AI tailwinds materialize and margins improve, while others caution about volatility and the stock's history of dramatic swings. Price targets vary widely, reflecting the high-risk, high-reward nature of the restructured entity.

The broader semiconductor sector has shown selective strength amid AI optimism, but Wolfspeed's move stands out for its magnitude. Year-to-date, the stock has rebounded dramatically from post-bankruptcy lows, though it remains well below pre-restructuring levels when factoring in massive dilution for legacy shareholders.

Technical traders noted the breakout above recent resistance with expanding volume. Support levels sit near $50-$53 from prior closes, while momentum indicators suggest potential for further upside if buying persists. However, profit-taking or short covering exhaustion could lead to sharp pullbacks given the stock's volatility.

For investors, Wolfspeed represents a speculative bet on the intersection of AI power demands and domestic semiconductor manufacturing. The company's ability to convert hype into consistent revenue growth and positive margins will be tested in coming quarters. Upcoming capacity milestones and potential new customer wins could sustain momentum.

Market watchers will closely monitor whether today's surge holds through the session or fades as a classic short-squeeze event. With high short interest and fresh AI narrative fuel, Wolfspeed remains one of the more volatile names in the semiconductor space heading into the second half of 2026.

value.Title: Earth Month 2026: How Everpure Employees Took Action Around the World

Document.Content:

Everpure

May 13th, 2026 ( 3BL Blogs - Delivered by Newstex )

During Earth Month 2026, Everpure deepened its commitment to sustainability as employees worldwide volunteered in initiatives focused on climate action, renewable energy education, ecosystem restoration, and circular economy projects.

In April, Everpure employees around the world came together to turn our sustainability values into action for Earth Month. In collaboration with the Everpure Foundation, teams hosted hands-on volunteer events, learning sessions, and local upcycling activities that made environmental stewardship tangible in offices and communities alike. Over 10% of our global employees participated in Earth Month activities, demonstrating our commitment to sustainability and a culture of purpose.

Renewable energy and climate education

Employees came together to provide tools for sustainable energy and empower the next generation with environmental knowledge.

Solar energy stations: Employees in Singapore and Raleigh-Durham, North Carolina, built stations to teach children about the mechanics and benefits of renewable energy for local nonprofit organizations Kampung Senang and El Futuro North Carolina.

Solar lamp construction: Teams in Lehi, Utah, assembled solar lamps to help Community Action Services & Food Bank promote the practical use of solar energy.

Climate action kits: In India, teammates created educational kits designed to engage local youth in climate advocacy, benefiting Bangalore-based Need Base India.

Virtual design-thinking challenge: Across the US, employees participated in a virtual hackathon hosted by Team4Tech, brainstorming solutions to support climate advocate user communities and stakeholder engagement.

Sustainability panel: Employees globally tuned in to hear from representatives throughout the company on how they're embedding sustainability into their corporate strategies.

Biodiversity and ecosystem restoration

This theme covered 'boots-on-the-ground' work to repair natural habitats and support local wildlife populations.

Reforestation: In Prague, employees collaborated with longtime partner Szme Stromy, planting trees to restore biodiversity on deforested land.

Seed ball creation: In Calgary, employees and customers worked together to create seed balls for CPAWS Southern Alberta, a nonprofit conservation organization working to promote thriving, healthy, and diverse lands and waters in Alberta.

Wildlife habitat protection: The Santa Clara team collaborated with South Bay Clean Creeks Coalition to remove trash and debris from Los Gatos Creek, specifically to support the health of local salmon and beaver populations.

Marine research: Employees throughout Europe analyzed images of the Great Barrier Reef, assisting Citizens of the Reef in their quest to deliver broad-scale data to reef managers globally.

Community cleanups: Employees in Dublin, Ireland; Lehi, Utah; and Staines, UK, rolled up their sleeves to clean up trash from their local parks.

Circular economy and digital footprint

In addition to volunteering, employees focused on reducing waste and making conscious shifts in daily habits.

Upcycling and composting: Employees in Prague and Santa Clara gave new life to old materials by upcycling clothing and backpacks.

E-waste and digital trash cleanup: Team members around the world dropped off old electronics for recycling and reduced their environmental footprint by taking out digital trash.

Sustainability panel: Subject matter experts from engineering, finance, supply chain, and more came together to discuss how they're embedding sustainability into their business strategies.

Earth Month celebrations culminated in a fireside chat with professional climber and climate advocate Alex Honnold, who shared his journey and how the Honnold Foundation is using community-scale solar projects to advance climate resilience and energy equity around the world. Employees rallied around these efforts, raising $20,000 for the Honnold Foundation's programs.

These efforts are part of a broader Everpure commitment to embed sustainability into how we work every day-from our people to our facilities to our products. By combining local volunteerism, listening sessions, and hands-on workshops, Everpure and the Everpure Foundation are helping employees turn climate concerns into concrete steps for people and the planet. As we close out Earth Month, one thing is clear: When our global community leans in together, small actions add up to meaningful, measurable impact.

Link to the original story.

value.Title: United States : Trump's crackdown on China -linked solar firms stalls U.S. factory boom

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-N943-S7BH-522V-00000-00United States: Trump's crackdown on China-linked solar firms stalls U.S. factory boom2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisUnited States: Trump's crackdown on China-linked solar firms stalls U.S. factory boomBangkokFULL TEXT

Top solar companies, banks and insurers have stopped doing business with at least a half dozen recently built U.S. panel factories because of uncertainty over whether their ties to China could disqualify them from clean-energy subsidies, according to industry executives and documents reviewed by Reuters.

The shift, driven by new Trump administration policies, jeopardizes more than a third of U.S. solar capacity in factories initially built by Chinese firms. Details of how the policy uncertainty is driving installers and insurers away from U.S. solar factories with China ties have not been previously reported.

The emerging effects dovetail with U.S. President Donald Trump's broader efforts to block Chinese companies from the U.S. market and to slash government support for green energy. However, the policy could backfire by imperiling growth in U.S. manufacturing jobs and power generation at a time of rising utility bills and soaring electricity demand from data centers serving the artificial intelligence industry, industry experts say.

Sunrun, the largest U.S. residential solar installer, is among the companies now avoiding Chinese suppliers.

It's holding up financings of desperately needed solar and storage projects, said Keith Martin, an attorney at Norton Rose Fulbright who advises on renewable energy tax deals.

The potentially far-reaching effects on U.S. manufacturing underscore the difficulty of decoupling from China's global dominance of renewable energy and green technologies, driven largely by Beijing's own heavy subsidies for Chinese firms.

The global reach of China's industrial policy creates a dilemma for U.S. regulators who want to block Chinese firms without imperiling U.S. solar manufacturers that depend on Chinese equipment and technology to produce competitive and affordable products.

Without robust growth in domestic solar manufacturing, the United States has few options for expanding renewable power beyond importing panels made by Chinese companies, which will lead to higher prices, U.S. executives say.

This is undoubtedly going to continue to increase the cost of power in the United States, said Aaron Halimi, chief executive of Renewable Properties, a San Francisco developer of small-scale utility projects that has shifted most of its sourcing to Tempe, Arizona-based First Solar to avoid suppliers with China links.

The fresh uncertainty in U.S. solar investments stems from provisions in the Trump-backed One Big Beautiful Bill that the Republican-controlled Congress passed in 2025.

The legislation slashed Biden-era clean-energy subsidies and restricted certain foreign countries, including China, from securing those that remained. The U.S. Treasury Department has yet to provide full guidance on how the law will be implemented, and a department spokesperson declined to give a timeline for when that guidance would be published.

Trump wants to rapidly expand the U.S. power grid to fuel American data centers. But power-industry experts say solar installations, combined with battery storage that clicks on when the sun isn't shining, are the quickest way to expand electricity generation because they're easier to build than gas, coal or nuclear plants.

Trump has called renewable energy unreliable and expensive and enacted policies promoting expansion of fossil fuel power sources.

The White House did not respond to a request for comment.

A spokesperson for China's embassy in Washington criticized the U.S. restrictions as discriminatory and said Beijing would defend its companies' interests.

CHINESE SOLAR FIRMS COMPLY - WITH A CATCH

China controls about 80% of global solar equipment manufacturing, according to Wood Mackenzie. Its companies, including LONGi, Trina, and others, were among the quickest to build and operate U.S. factories when former President Joe Biden's 2022 climate-change law created a tax credit for clean-energy factories.

Since then, solar equipment makers have announced nearly $43 billion in investments supporting a projected 48,000 jobs, according to the Solar Energy Industries Association.

Domestic manufacturing is now aligned with U.S. demand for solar panels, eliminating the need for panel imports. But that could change if a significant portion of U.S. factories caught up in the regulatory uncertainty are unable to compete.

The Trump-backed legislation restricts Chinese companies to 25% ownership stakes in plants seeking federal subsidies, imposes sourcing requirements, and prohibits effective control by Chinese firms. Companies say the subsidies, which include tax credits for solar manufacturing and installation, are crucial to remaining competitive.

Chinese companies have sought to comply by selling off factory stakes or otherwise restructuring. But most have preserved financial links to their U.S. plants, sometimes in the form of profit-sharing or supply deals, according to a Reuters review of corporate disclosures.

Industry officials have questions about whether those remaining links disqualify the factories from U.S. clean energy manufacturing credits. Absent guidance from the Treasury Department, installers including industry behemoth Sunrun are shunning these factories, while banks and insurers are withholding financing and coverage.

PARING BACK APPROVED SUPPLIERS

Sunrun in January circulated a pared-down list of approved solar-panel suppliers to installation partners, according to a document seen by Reuters.

The list included only non-Chinese manufacturers such as Qcells, REC, Silfab and Elin. Previously, it had included Canadian Solar, JA Solar, Jinko, LONGi and Trina - all of which are China-linked.

We have taken a conservative stance and do not procure equipment from manufacturers that would raise compliance concerns, Sunrun Deputy Chief Financial Officer Patrick Jobin said in a statement to Reuters.

Palmetto, a North Carolina-based company that sells rooftop solar panels, is also steering clear of China-linked producers despite their attempts at compliance, general manager Sean Hayes said.

Meanwhile, banks including Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs have scaled back tax-equity financing for some solar projects due to concerns that future Treasury interpretations could retroactively invalidate tax credits, according to three people familiar with the deals who spoke on condition of anonymity.

The banks declined to comment.

Insurers have taken a harder line, refusing to insure companies against the risk they will be barred from clean-energy tax credits, according to Antony Joyce, a tax-insurance specialist at broker Marsh.

The companies that are best positioned right now are certainly the ones that didn't have clear ownership ties to a country of concern, said Peter Henderson, a principal at accounting firm Baker Tilly, who said Treasury's expected guidance will be crucial.

The Solar Energy Manufacturers for America Coalition, a trade group representing non-Chinese companies with U.S. factories, including First Solar and Hanwha's Qcells , has urged the Treasury Department to take a tough stance.

'CLEAREST WAY TO COMPLY'

The core issue driving firms away is that Chinese companies are maintaining ties with their factories instead of making a clean break. Factories that were originally built and operated by China-linked producers account for at least 25 gigawatts of the nation's about 66 GW of operating solar module manufacturing capacity.

Very few Chinese manufacturers are actually decoupling themselves from their U.S. factories entirely, said Elissa Pierce, an analyst at Wood Mackenzie.

China's JinkoSolar, which operates a factory in Florida, and the Chinese parent company of Boviet Solar, which produces panels in North Carolina, have said they are looking for outside investors.

Illuminate USA, a joint venture between China's LONGi and Chicago-based Invenergy, reduced the Chinese firm's ownership stake in an Ohio plant built in 2024 to below 25% and renegotiated its intellectual property agreement with LONGi, according to an Invenergy source.

But Invenergy is unsure if the plant, which employs around 1,700 workers, will survive. Illuminate and LONGi did not comment.

In March comments to the Internal Revenue Service urging clear guidance, the company said: The continued operation of Illuminate USA and other U.S. manufacturers remains at risk.

Source: DD News

value.Title: Labcorp Publishes 2025 Corporate Responsibility Report, Highlighting Progress in Sustainability, Access to Care and Employee Well-Being

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-FN43-S35T-Y4RB-00000-00Labcorp Publishes 2025 Corporate Responsibility Report, Highlighting Progress in Sustainability, Access to Care and Employee Well-Being2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisLabcorp Publishes 2025 Corporate Responsibility Report, Highlighting Progress in Sustainability, Access to Care and Employee Well-Being

Key Highlights:

* Launched a global employee recognition program with 443,000 acts of recognition awards.

* Increased minimum hourly wage for U.S.-based employees to $17.75 per hour.

* Expanded hybrid/electric vehicles in fleet to 2,500, improving fuel efficiency by 10%.

Original Press Release:

May 7 -- Labcorp issued the following news release:

Labcorp (NYSE: LH), a global leader of innovative and comprehensive laboratory services, today announced the release of its 2025 Corporate Responsibility Report, demonstrating advancements in expanding access to care, sustainable operations, employee development, ethical governance and community impact.

"Labcorp's commitment to operate responsibly directly connects to our mission to improve health and improve lives," said Tracy Strong, Chief Compliance, Privacy & Sustainability Officer. "In 2025, we made meaningful progress toward our sustainable operations goals, deepened our focus on ethics and compliance programs, invested in our people and enhanced access to important healthcare and education initiatives for our communities. Our progress wouldn't be possible without the dedication of our employees who are committed to helping us operate responsibly and advance health today and tomorrow."

Report highlights include:

Workforce investment and development

* Launched a global employee recognition program that provided 443,000 acts of recognition awards including 193,000 monetary awards

*Increased our minimum hourly wage for all U.S.-based, non-union employees to $17.75 per hour, representing a $20 million investment in our frontline workforce

*Continued upfront tuition coverage for degree and certificate programs in healthcare fields of study through the Labcorp Education Advantage program, enabling more than 3,900 employees to pursue higher education since its inception

Achievements in sustainable operations

* Earned a Silver EcoVadis rating, along with an A- rating from CDP Climate and an A on the Supplier Engagement Assessment (SEA), reflecting strong governance, transparency and progress in managing environmental impacts

*Expanded hybrid/electric vehicles in our fleet to more than 2,500 vehicles, improving fuel efficiency by 10% versus 2021

*Operated 100% of Biopharma Laboratory Services labs on renewable electricity and/or renewable energy certificates

Expanding access to care and strengthening communities

* Increased access to clinical trials through remote sampling capabilities in alternate collection facilities or in the comfort of a patient's home

*Awarded more than 190 grants through The Labcorp Charitable Foundation to support health, education and initiatives for underserved populations globally

*Expanded breast cancer screening for uninsured populations in North Carolina and strengthened pediatric oncology services for rural communities in China through a partnership between The Labcorp Charitable Foundation and Project HOPE

Read the full 2025 Corporate Responsibility Report here(https://www.labcorp.com/content/dam/labcorp/files/about/2001700-0326-1_BRO_Corporate%20responsibility%20report%202025_FINAL.pdf).

About Labcorp

Labcorp (NYSE: LH) is a global leader of innovative and comprehensive laboratory services that helps doctors, hospitals, pharmaceutical companies, researchers and patients make clear and confident decisions. We provide insights and advance science to improve health and improve lives through our unparalleled diagnostics and drug development laboratory capabilities. The company's nearly 71,000 employees serve clients in approximately 100 countries, provided support for more than 85% of the new drugs and therapeutic products approved by the FDA in 2025 and performed more than 750 million tests for patients around the world. Learn more at www.labcorp.com

[Category: Healthcare, ESG and Sustainability, Sustainability Initiatives, CSR, Corporate Governance]

Source: Labcorp

urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSVN-10BT-00000-00How Dominion Energy Has Fared Since Its Recent Analyst Ratings Changes *Updated*2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisHow Dominion Energy Has Fared Since Its Recent Analyst Ratings Changes *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

Dominion Energy (NYSE:D), the NYSE's 2nd largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, is up 11% Year to Date. There have been changes in analyst ratings and target prices in 2026 - some forward-looking and others that have been after price movements. Below are the recent analyst ratings changes and the change in company stock price since.

Rating DateAnalyst FirmRatingTarget PriceReturn Since Change (%)21 Apr, 2026Morgan Stanleyto Equal Weight69 to 685.615 Apr, 2026Bank of Americato Neutral63 to 653.410 Apr, 2026Jefferies Financial Groupto Hold60 to 650.408 Apr, 2026Barclaysto Overweight63 to 662.102 Mar, 2026ScotiabankSector Perform63 to 672.324 Feb, 2026MizuhoNeutral62 to 662.322 Jan, 2026BarclaysOverweight64 to 638.820 Jan, 2026Wells Fargo & CompanyOverweight67 to 646.709 Jan, 2026TD CowenHold6512.417 Dec, 2025BarclaysOverweight63 to 648.5

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

value.Title: Earth Month 2026: How Everpure Employees Took Action Around the World

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-Y3K3-RVJB-015T-00000-00Earth Month 2026: How Everpure Employees Took Action Around the World2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisEarth Month 2026: How Everpure Employees Took Action Around the World

May 13, 2026

During Earth Month 2026, Everpure deepened its commitment to sustainability as employees worldwide volunteered in initiatives focused on climate action, renewable energy education, ecosystem restoration, and circular economy projects.

In April, Everpure employees around the world came together to turn our sustainability values into action for Earth Month. In collaboration with the Everpure Foundation, teams hosted hands-on volunteer events, learning sessions, and local upcycling activities that made environmental stewardship tangible in offices and communities alike. Over 10% of our global employees participated in Earth Month activities, demonstrating our commitment to sustainability and a culture of purpose.Renewable energy and climate education

Employees came together to provide tools for sustainable energy and empower the next generation with environmental knowledge.



* Solar energy stations: Employees in Singapore and Raleigh-Durham, North Carolina, built stations to teach children about the mechanics and benefits of renewable energy for local nonprofit organizations Kampung Senang and El Futuro North Carolina.

* Solar lamp construction: Teams in Lehi, Utah, assembled solar lamps to help Community Action Services Food Bank promote the practical use of solar energy.

* Climate action kits: In India, teammates created educational kits designed to engage local youth in climate advocacy, benefiting Bangalore-based Need Base India.

* Virtual design-thinking challenge: Across the US, employees participated in a virtual hackathon hosted by Team4Tech, brainstorming solutions to support climate advocate user communities and stakeholder engagement.

* Sustainability panel: Employees globally tuned in to hear from representatives throughout the company on how they're embedding sustainability into their corporate strategies.

Biodiversity and ecosystem restoration

This theme covered "boots-on-the-ground" work to repair natural habitats and support local wildlife populations.

* Reforestation: In Prague, employees collaborated with longtime partner Sázíme Stromy, planting trees to restore biodiversity on deforested land.

* Seed ball creation: In Calgary, employees and customers worked together to create seed balls for CPAWS Southern Alberta, a nonprofit conservation organization working to promote thriving, healthy, and diverse lands and waters in Alberta.

* Wildlife habitat protection: The Santa Clara team collaborated with South Bay Clean Creeks Coalition to remove trash and debris from Los Gatos Creek, specifically to support the health of local salmon and beaver populations.

* Marine research: Employees throughout Europe analyzed images of the Great Barrier Reef, assisting Citizens of the Reef in their quest to deliver broad-scale data to reef managers globally.

* Community cleanups: Employees in Dublin, Ireland; Lehi, Utah; and Staines, UK, rolled up their sleeves to clean up trash from their local parks.

Circular economy and digital footprint

In addition to volunteering, employees focused on reducing waste and making conscious shifts in daily habits.

* Upcycling and composting: Employees in Prague and Santa Clara gave new life to old materials by upcycling clothing and backpacks.

* E-waste and digital trash cleanup: Team members around the world dropped off old electronics for recycling and reduced their environmental footprint by taking out digital trash.

* Sustainability panel: Subject matter experts from engineering, finance, supply chain, and more came together to discuss how they're embedding sustainability into their business strategies.



Earth Month celebrations culminated in a fireside chat with professional climber and climate advocate Alex Honnold, who shared his journey and how the Honnold Foundation is using community-scale solar projects to advance climate resilience and energy equity around the world. Employees rallied around these efforts, raising $ 20,000 for the Honnold Foundation's programs.

These efforts are part of a broader Everpure commitment to embed sustainability into how we work every day-from our people to our facilities to our products. By combining local volunteerism, listening sessions, and hands-on workshops, Everpure and the Everpure Foundation are helping employees turn climate concerns into concrete steps for people and the planet. As we close out Earth Month, one thing is clear: When our global community leans in together, small actions add up to meaningful, measurable impact.

value.Jurisdiction: Canada; International; North Carolina; Utah
value.Title: Earth Month 2026: How Everpure Employees Took Action Around the World

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-Y3K3-RVJB-00X5-00000-00Earth Month 2026: How Everpure Employees Took Action Around the World2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisEarth Month 2026: How Everpure Employees Took Action Around the World

During Earth Month 2026, Everpure deepened its commitment to sustainability as employees worldwide volunteered in initiatives focused on climate action, renewable energy education, ecosystem restoration, and circular economy projects.

In April, Everpure employees around the world came together to turn our sustainability values into action for Earth Month. In collaboration with the Everpure Foundation, teams hosted hands-on volunteer events, learning sessions, and local upcycling activities that made environmental stewardship tangible in offices and communities alike. Over 10% of our global employees participated in Earth Month activities, demonstrating our commitment to sustainability and a culture of purpose.Renewable energy and climate education

Employees came together to provide tools for sustainable energy and empower the next generation with environmental knowledge.



* Solar energy stations: Employees in Singapore and Raleigh-Durham, North Carolina, built stations to teach children about the mechanics and benefits of renewable energy for local nonprofit organizations Kampung Senang and El Futuro North Carolina.

* Solar lamp construction: Teams in Lehi, Utah, assembled solar lamps to help Community Action Services Food Bank promote the practical use of solar energy.

* Climate action kits: In India, teammates created educational kits designed to engage local youth in climate advocacy, benefiting Bangalore-based Need Base India.

* Virtual design-thinking challenge: Across the US, employees participated in a virtual hackathon hosted by Team4Tech, brainstorming solutions to support climate advocate user communities and stakeholder engagement.

* Sustainability panel: Employees globally tuned in to hear from representatives throughout the company on how they're embedding sustainability into their corporate strategies.

Biodiversity and ecosystem restoration

This theme covered "boots-on-the-ground" work to repair natural habitats and support local wildlife populations.

* Reforestation: In Prague, employees collaborated with longtime partner Sázíme Stromy, planting trees to restore biodiversity on deforested land.

* Seed ball creation: In Calgary, employees and customers worked together to create seed balls for CPAWS Southern Alberta, a nonprofit conservation organization working to promote thriving, healthy, and diverse lands and waters in Alberta.

* Wildlife habitat protection: The Santa Clara team collaborated with South Bay Clean Creeks Coalition to remove trash and debris from Los Gatos Creek, specifically to support the health of local salmon and beaver populations.

* Marine research: Employees throughout Europe analyzed images of the Great Barrier Reef, assisting Citizens of the Reef in their quest to deliver broad-scale data to reef managers globally.

* Community cleanups: Employees in Dublin, Ireland; Lehi, Utah; and Staines, UK, rolled up their sleeves to clean up trash from their local parks.

Circular economy and digital footprint

In addition to volunteering, employees focused on reducing waste and making conscious shifts in daily habits.

* Upcycling and composting: Employees in Prague and Santa Clara gave new life to old materials by upcycling clothing and backpacks.

* E-waste and digital trash cleanup: Team members around the world dropped off old electronics for recycling and reduced their environmental footprint by taking out digital trash.

* Sustainability panel: Subject matter experts from engineering, finance, supply chain, and more came together to discuss how they're embedding sustainability into their business strategies.



Earth Month celebrations culminated in a fireside chat with professional climber and climate advocate Alex Honnold, who shared his journey and how the Honnold Foundation is using community-scale solar projects to advance climate resilience and energy equity around the world. Employees rallied around these efforts, raising $ 20,000 for the Honnold Foundation's programs.

These efforts are part of a broader Everpure commitment to embed sustainability into how we work every day-from our people to our facilities to our products. By combining local volunteerism, listening sessions, and hands-on workshops, Everpure and the Everpure Foundation are helping employees turn climate concerns into concrete steps for people and the planet. As we close out Earth Month, one thing is clear: When our global community leans in together, small actions add up to meaningful, measurable impact.



(Distributed by M2 Communications (www.m2.co.uk))

urn:contentItem:6JHK-SCK3-S7MT-6167-00000-00NextEra Energy May Explore Clean Energy Leadership Opportunities Following Duke Energy's Tanya Hall v. Duke Energy Coal Ash Cancer Cluster Litigation *Updated*2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisNextEra Energy May Explore Clean Energy Leadership Opportunities Following Duke Energy's Tanya Hall v. Duke Energy Coal Ash Cancer Cluster Litigation *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know

This legal proceeding underscores existing inquiries into historical coal ash management and its possible connection to community health conditions within the utilities sector. Stakeholders are advised to observe how these matters might shape regulatory frameworks concerning environmental remediation, disclosure requirements, and the management of long-term liabilities. The results could initiate comprehensive industry evaluations of legacy infrastructure protocols, which may impact compliance expenditures and public confidence. Competitive consequences involve adjustments in utility positioning throughout the energy transition, specifically concerning environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics and community engagement. This situation highlights the importance of preemptive risk evaluations across comparable operational areas domestically.

Background of the Case

The litigation, Tanya Hall v. Duke Energy Carolinas, LLC (24CV045806-590), commenced on October 2, 2024, in the Mecklenburg County Superior Court of North Carolina. The plaintiff, Tanya Hall, claims that the handling and disposal of coal ash from an unlined basin at the Marshall Steam Station resulted in exposure to contaminants, leading to a kidney cancer diagnosis in May 2023. The filing alleges negligence, unfair trade practices, fraudulent concealment, and ultra-hazardous operations, asserting that Duke Energy withheld data regarding health risks while utilizing coal ash as fill material near residential zones during the 1990s and 2000s. The proceedings focus on operations near Sherrills Ford and Lake Norman, where local health inquiries have led to requests for Environmental Protection Agency involvement. No specific financial damages are publicly outlined, but the filing could precede additional claims. Duke Energy has indicated it is unaware of scientific data connecting its operations to the alleged health effects and maintains it has operated in compliance with prior regulatory standards. The litigation is currently pending, with discovery processes underway.

Competitive Impacts on NextEra Energy

With a substantial renewable energy portfolio and limited reliance on coal-fired generation, NextEra Energy may experience indirect market shifts resulting from increased sector oversight of legacy coal ash matters. The company's market position could benefit as investors and consumers increasingly favor utilities maintaining cleaner environmental profiles. Public attention on community health may accelerate the transition toward low-carbon alternatives, potentially improving NextEra's standing in competitive bidding for large-scale infrastructure projects. Nevertheless, subsequent regulatory changes could introduce uniform compliance expenses across the sector, necessitating that NextEra allocate resources toward advanced ESG reporting to sustain its current market position.

Possible Opportunities for NextEra Energy

Leverage reputational positioning by advancing renewable project development in regions where competitors are managing legacy infrastructure challenges.

Enhance investor relations via comprehensive reporting of environmental risk management protocols.

Develop strategic collaborations with municipalities prioritizing the transition to clean energy.

Increase market presence among data centers and corporate clients requiring sustainable energy providers.

Why This Case Is Challenging for NextEra Energy

Indirect regulatory developments stemming from this litigation could raise compliance requirements for all utilities, potentially increasing operational expenditures even for entities with minimal coal ash involvement. Sector-wide legal precedents regarding toxic torts may elevate litigation risks for any documented environmental deviations. Increased public and investor attention on health-related claims could amplify ESG oversight, prompting NextEra to allocate additional capital toward transparency initiatives. Furthermore, any mandated industry-wide remediation frameworks might redirect funds from growth-oriented projects, while competitive dynamics adjust toward utilities assessed as lower-risk on environmental performance metrics.

Key Challenges for NextEra Energy

Possible increases in sector compliance expenses impacting capital allocation.

Expanded ESG reporting requirements to address increased public oversight.

Potential for litigation precedents to affect future environmental proceedings.

Competitive dynamics in regions prioritizing accelerated clean energy integration.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Allocate capital to advanced environmental monitoring and community outreach initiatives.

Improve sustainability disclosures to emphasize renewable energy capabilities.

Evaluate acquisitions or partnerships that reinforce clean energy infrastructure.

Engage in policy advocacy for regulatory frameworks that facilitate the energy transition.

Key Measures

NextEra Energy could execute these strategies via directed capital allocation toward grid modernization and stakeholder engagement programs, ensuring integration with current operations while maintaining its standing as an industry standard for responsible energy development.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Improved brand perception among ESG investors prioritizing lower environmental risk portfolios.

Increased customer acquisition rates in regions prioritizing renewable energy.

Avenues for policy engagement supporting clean technology integration.

Enhanced recruitment capabilities within sustainability-focused labor markets.

Potential Losses

Margin compression resulting from potential sector-wide increases in compliance expenditures.

Extended project timelines caused by comprehensive regulatory evaluations.

Reputational impact if industry-wide environmental inquiries expand.

Elevated cost of capital if cautious investor sentiment extends across the entire utilities sector.

Final Thoughts

The Tanya Hall litigation offers NextEra Energy a strategic interval to solidify its position in clean energy as the broader sector addresses legacy environmental matters. By utilizing its renewable portfolio and proactive governance frameworks, NextEra can translate indirect industry pressures into distinct market positioning. Operational success will depend on the disciplined implementation of transparency programs and focused growth strategies that correspond with changing stakeholder requirements across the utilities industry.

Tags: Tanya Hall v. Duke Energy Corp. (Coal Ash Cancer Cluster)

Activities

Duke Energy Corporation is a leading U.S.-based energy company, traded on the New York Stock Exchange, that specializes in the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, as well as natural gas services. Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, the company serves approximately 8 million customers across six states, primarily through its regulated utilities in the Carolinas, Florida, and the Midwest. Its core operations focus on reliable power supply from a diverse mix of sources, including coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewables. The fastest-growing segments include renewable energy initiatives, such as solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and electric vehicle charging infrastructure are driving growth, positioning Duke Energy as a key player in the transition to a more sustainable energy future. It is the NYSE's largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Duke Energy Corporation (NYSE:DUK) is a leading force in the energy sector, renowned for its commitment to environmental stewardship through initiatives such as the Duke Energy Foundation America250 grants. The company actively collaborates on the America250 grants South Carolina and the Duke Energy America250 initiative, focusing on Green space protection South Carolina. Through Corporate philanthropy Duke Energy, the company supports Duke Energy grants South Carolina green spaces and South Carolina conservation grants. Their Duke Energy Foundation grants underpin strategic efforts in Habitat restoration grants South Carolina and Conservation grants Duke Energy. They provide Land conservation grants South Carolina and Coastal resilience grants South Carolina, advancing Duke Energy community resilience grants and South Carolina habitat protection grants. Urban green space grants Duke Energy and Environmental education grants Duke Energy foster sustainable growth. As Duke Energy Foundation grant recipients, stakeholders benefit from Duke Energy nonprofit grants and the America250 partnership Duke Energy Foundation. The Duke Energy environmental grants program and Duke Energy philanthropic grants emphasize conservation easements South Carolina, supported by South Carolina parks funding Duke Energy and Duke Energy community grants, strengthening Duke Energy corporate giving South Carolina. Duke Energy volunteer conservation programs and Green infrastructure grants Duke Energy illustrate their dedication to sustainability and community resilience.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-RPJ3-S1N7-G2NN-00000-002.3 million metric tons of lithium hidden beneath the Appalachian Mountains could power the US for centuries: Study2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisREST OF WORLD2.3 million metric tons of lithium hidden beneath the Appalachian Mountains could power the US for centuries: StudyTOI World Desk

The United States Geological Survey (USGS) identified undiscovered, economically recoverable lithium in the Appalachian Mountains, with 2.3 million metric tons of lithium oxide. This discovery holds a value of approximately 90 billion dollars and marks a vital shift for energy security in the US. These reserves are mostly in the 'Tin-Spodumene Belt' of the Carolinas, extending towards the northeast.

There is enough to meet the nation's growing needs for centuries for many generations. With global demand for battery minerals soaring, this discovery provides a chance to cut back on imports from countries like China and Chile. It's not just about boosting the American electric vehicle market; it also strengthens the country's resolve towards a future less dependent on foreign minerals.USGS identifies a lithium-rich region in Appalachia as a key resource for the USThe USGS has recently pointed out that the Appalachian region holds a large amount of spodumene, which is a mineral containing lithium found in certain types of rocks. In the 2024 update, the US critical minerals list names lithium as crucial for both national security and economic growth. According to the journal published by the US Geological Survey, the United States imports more than 25 per cent of its lithium needs. Therefore, this discovery of 2.3 million tons could become a key strategic resource for the country.Historical legacy of the Tin-Spodumene Belt Lithium can be found in the Tin-Spodumene Belt, which extends from South Carolina into North Carolina. The Department of Energy's historical records show that during the mid-20th century, this region was the world's top lithium source. Recent studies by the National Renewable Energy Laboratory emphasise the importance of reopening these mines to help achieve the Biden-Harris administration's target of 50 per cent electric vehicle sales by 2030.The environmental shift in lithium extractionThe International Energy Agency (IEA) predicts a 40-fold increase in global lithium demand by 2040. A study published in the National Institutes of Health discusses how extracting lithium from hard-rock pegmatites in areas like the Appalachians affects the environment differently than using brine evaporation ponds. By tapping into these local resources, around 130 million electric vehicle batteries could be powered, which would greatly reduce the carbon emissions tied to shipping minerals from overseas.US policy is incentivising local mineral extractionThe Federal Consortium for Advanced Batteries (FCAB) emphasises the importance of establishing a complete domestic supply chain, from mining operations in the Appalachians to recycling processes. This focus is crucial for the government sector. Moreover, this initiative aligns with the Inflation Reduction Act's stipulations on sourcing minerals domestically to be eligible for clean vehicle tax credits. The Internal Revenue Service and the Department of the Treasury have outlined these requirements clearly. For Reprint Rights: timescontent.com

urn:contentItem:6JHD-M503-S6WK-11X7-00000-00How SunPower Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisHow SunPower Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

SunPower Corporation (NASDAQ: SPWR)

- Company Profile: SunPower Corporation (NASDAQ: SPWR) is a U.S.-headquartered solar energy firm that manufactures solar panels, with a global workforce of around 2,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1.1 billion capital injection in 2025 is set for the expansion of solar panel manufacturing in California.

- Recipient: SunPower U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Renewable Energy, Manufacturing

Avnet Inc. (NASDAQ: AVT)

- Company Profile: Avnet Inc. (NASDAQ: AVT) is a U.S.-based technology distributor of semiconductors and technology components, employing around 10,000 people globally.

Activities

SunPower Corporation, a NASDAQ-listed company, is a leading provider of high-efficiency solar technology and solutions, specializing in the design, manufacturing, and installation of photovoltaic systems for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications. The company's core activities encompass the development of advanced solar panels, energy storage systems, and integrated software for monitoring and optimizing renewable energy use, supporting the global transition to sustainable power sources. Among its fastest-growing segments are residential solar installations, fueled by rising consumer demand for clean energy and supportive government incentives, as well as commercial energy storage solutions that enhance grid reliability and cost savings. SunPower's innovative focus on high-performance products and digital platforms positions it to capitalize on the expanding solar market, driving efficiency and reducing carbon footprints for its customers. It is the NASDAQ's 24th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Tilray Brands Inc., NASDAQ:TLRY, offers a comprehensive range of products in the cannabis industry with a focus on providing high-quality experiences. Their product line includes Tilray CBD oil and Tilray THC vape cartridges, catering to diverse needs. The Tilray cannabis gummies and Tilray cannabis tinctures provide edibles for those exploring new consumption formats. For those preferring traditional options, Tilray pre-rolls and Tilray dried cannabis flower are available, along with Tilray THC edibles tailored for various preferences. Their extensive portfolio covers everything from Tilray medical cannabis products to Tilray adult-use cannabis products, enhancing accessibility for different user groups.

Incorporating wellness solutions, Tilray offers Tilray CBD topicals, Tilray CBD softgels, and even Tilray CBD pet products, extending their reach into pet care. Their collaboration with Manitoba Harvest brings Manitoba Harvest hemp foods including Manitoba Harvest hemp protein, to health-conscious consumers. They further expand into beverages with Tilray infused beverages and Tilray cannabis beverages, complemented by SweetWater craft beer (Tilray).

Their innovation in devices and concentrates is evident with Tilray vape pens & cartridges, Tilray cannabis concentrates, Tilray live resin, Tilray hash rosin, and an array of Tilray extraction products. Tilray cultivation genetics & strains underline their commitment to quality, while Tilray branded pre-roll packs and Tilray bulk THC cartridges cater to consumer needs. Additionally, Tilray CBD isolate powder and Tilray full-spectrum CBD oil showcase their expertise in product development.

With a robust presence in the Tilray U.S. cannabis distribution network, Tilray product availability U.S. ensures broad access across the region. Tilray cannabis retail brands provide a comprehensive marketplace for consumers to explore. Tilray continues to be a frontrunner in innovation and customer satisfaction in the cannabis sector.

urn:contentItem:6JGJ-JTH3-RSH0-R13D-00000-00Siemens AG (NYSE:SIEGY (ADR)) Secures €40 Million Framework Agreement With Vulcan Energy for Lionheart Lithium Project2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisSiemens AG (NYSE:SIEGY (ADR)) Secures €40 Million Framework Agreement With Vulcan Energy for Lionheart Lithium Project

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

01 May 2026 12:16 EDT

Siemens AG (NYSE:SIEGY (ADR)) shares rose about 1.5 percent to €250.35 on Wednesday as the group outlined a slate of strategic moves, including a €40 million framework agreement with Vulcan Energy for the Lionheart lithium project in the Upper Rhine Graben. Siemens will provide automation, engineering, and telecommunications services for the Landau extraction facility and the Frankfurt-Hochst processing plant. The initial order totals €40 million, while Siemens Financial Services will commit €67 million toward the project’s total cost of €2.2 billion, a venture aimed at producing lithium hydroxide from geothermal brine while generating renewable energy, ahead of the second-quarter earnings report on 13th May 2026

In parallel, Siemens Mobility has completed a USD $220 million manufacturing facility in Lexington, North Carolina. The plant commenced operations in April 2026, with the first 25 passenger railcars rolling off the production line. The facility is expected to create about 500 jobs by 2028, reinforcing Siemens’ U.S. rail manufacturing footprint

CEO Roland Busch is steering an internal restructuring that could dissolve Digital Industries and Smart Infrastructure to form six or seven smaller units under a unified One Tech Company framework. The Mobility division remains unaffected, with supervisory board discussions with worker representatives anticipated in May 2026. Analysts have begun to shift to a more positive stance, with HSBC upgrading the stock to Buy from Hold and lifting the target to €300, while Bernstein notes Siemens’ ongoing €6 billion share buyback is nearing completion and could be followed by at least €10 billion more

SECTION 1 SIEMENS ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Siemens Activities

Activities

Siemens AG, headquartered in Munich, Germany, is a global leader in technology and engineering, specializing in electrification, automation, and digitalization. The company's core business activities span diverse sectors, including energy solutions, healthcare systems, industrial software, mobility infrastructure, and smart building technologies. Siemens delivers innovative products and services that enhance efficiency, sustainability, and connectivity across industries. Among its fastest-growing segments are digital industries, driven by demand for AI-powered automation and IoT solutions in manufacturing; sustainable energy initiatives, such as wind power and grid management systems; and healthcare advancements, including advanced imaging and diagnostic tools. This strategic focus on high-growth areas enables Siemens to maintain a competitive edge, fostering long-term value through technological innovation and global partnerships. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's largest Miscellaneous manufacturing company by market capitalisation.
